reasoning: The FRED data indicates that the value of EXPINF2YR as of December 2025 has spiked to 2.742%, which matches the upper end of its recent historical range (fluctuating between 2.26% and 2.61% for most of 2025). Provided that the series tends to be mean-reverting around ~2.50%, the probability of a further increase from this exceptionally high reading in January 2026 is relatively low. The FRED trend analysis tool estimates roughly a 0.28 probability of the January 2026 value being strictly greater than 2.742%. For the March 2026 resolution, the forecast horizon is further out, which intrinsically increases uncertainty; additionally, with more time, a longer-term drift or macro shifts could happen. Thus, the probability estimate is pulled slightly closer to 0.5 (0.34) for the March 2026 resolution.
